Deploying a deep learning model designed in Python on AWS requires careful planning and execution. * * *  
The model must be packaged with dependencies and deployed via AWS Lambda or SageMaker, ensuring scalability and performance. * * *  
Data security and model overfitting are critical considerations, requiring proper encryption and validation techniques. * * *  
The model must be validated and tested in a production-like environment to ensure reliability and accuracy. * * *

The model must be validated and tested in a production-like environment to ensure reliability and accuracy. * * *  
The deployment process involves setting up an AWS S3 bucket for storage and using IAM roles for access control. * * *  
Model overfitting can occur if the training data is not representative, leading to poor generalization on new data. * * *  
AWS provides tools like XGBoost and TensorFlow Serving to manage model deployment and scaling efficiently. * * *
